title: [FIRRTL] Inconsistent Constant Propagation Behavior with Zero-Width Signals
body:
I've encountered what appears to be an inconsistency in `firtool`'s constant propagation when zero-width signals are involved.

## Reproducer

My `firtool` was built from a recent checkout a few days ago; any recent version may reproduce the same behavior.

```shell
$ firtool --version
LLVM (http://llvm.org/):
  LLVM version 22.0.0git
  DEBUG build with assertions.
CIRCT 24ad90a75
```

Below are two minimal FIRRTL examples, `demo1.fir` and `demo2.fir`.

`demo1.fir`

```firrtl
FIRRTL version 3.2.0
circuit demo1:
    module demo1:
        input a: UInt<0>
        output b: UInt<1>

        wire r: UInt<0>
        connect r,  a
        connect b, pad(r, 1)
```

`demo2.fir`

```firrtl
FIRRTL version 3.2.0
circuit demo2:
    module demo2:
        input a: UInt<0>
        output b: UInt<1>

        wire r: UInt<0>
        connect r, a
        connect b, tail(add(pad(r, 1), UInt<1>(1)), 1)
```

In my intuition, since `a` and `r` have type `UInt<0>`:

- In `demo1`, `pad(r, 1)` should always evaluate to `0`, so `b` should be constant `0`.

- In `demo2`, `pad(r, 1)` is `0`, so `add(0, 1)` yields `1`, and `tail(..., 1)` preserves that value; thus `b` should be constant `1`.

## Observed Behavior

Running `firtool` with `--ir-fir` produces different optimization results:

```shell
$ firtool --ir-fir demo1.fir
```

```mlir
module {
  firrtl.circuit "demo1" {
    firrtl.module @demo1(in %a: !firrtl.uint<0>, out %b: !firrtl.uint<1>) attributes {convention = #firrtl<convention scalarized>} {
      %0 = firrtl.pad %a, 1 : (!firrtl.uint<0>) -> !firrtl.uint<1>
      firrtl.matchingconnect %b, %0 : !firrtl.uint<1>
    }
  }
}
```

```
$ firtool --ir-fir demo2.fir
```

```mlir
module {
  firrtl.circuit "demo2" {
    firrtl.module @demo2(in %a: !firrtl.uint<0>, out %b: !firrtl.uint<1>) attributes {convention = #firrtl<convention scalarized>} {
      %c1_ui1 = firrtl.constant 1 : !firrtl.uint<1>
      firrtl.matchingconnect %b, %c1_ui1 : !firrtl.uint<1>
    }
  }
}
```

As shown above:
 
- `demo2` is optimized as expected, with `b` recognized as the constant `1`.

- `demo1`, however, is left unoptimized, and `b` is not recognized as a constant `0`.

## Question

Could someone help clarify why constant propagation succeeds in `demo2` but not in `demo1`?

Thanks in advance for any insight. This is a great project, and I appreciate your time and work on `firtool`.
